about.title: A Philosophy of Freedom
about.p1: Livingston opened on October 4, 2019, and from its very first day claimed a unique place on Armenia's gastronomic map. The restaurant's concept is inspired by Richard Bach's novel "Jonathan Livingston Seagull" — a story about freedom, the pursuit of perfection, and the desire to transcend the limits of what is possible.
about.p2: Located on the 9th floor of the Sports and Concert Complex, the restaurant offers panoramic views of Yerevan — a city that reveals itself from an entirely new perspective from here. Every detail has been considered: from the table setting to the final chord of the jazz orchestra.

cuisine.title: Haute Cuisine
cuisine.subtitle: European haute cuisine at Livingston means signature dishes built on a balance of flavors, impeccable technique, and respect for every ingredient.

dishes.carpaccio_desc: With mustard cream, capers and parmesan
dishes.lamb_desc: With pak choi and red wine jus
dishes.chicken_desc: A French classic in an author's interpretation
dishes.turkey_desc: With tonnato sauce and crispy capers

atmosphere.title: An Evening to Remember
atmosphere.subtitle: Every evening at Livingston is more than dinner. It is a performance where music, light and the view of nocturnal Yerevan play the leading roles.
atmosphere.jazz_desc: Under the baton of Maestro Yervand Yerznkyan — classics of the 1960s–90s performed live
atmosphere.dj_desc: Contemporary club music from Yerevan's finest artists
atmosphere.prog3_desc: Exclusive tasting menu by the head chef with wine pairing
atmosphere.prog4_desc: Relaxed setting, soft lighting and gentle background music
atmosphere.prog5_desc: Intimate dinners, business meetings and private celebrations

events.title: A Space for Special Occasions
events.subtitle: Livingston hosts corporate dinners, wedding receptions and official state events. The hall accommodates up to 150 guests.
